subject: [PATCH 5/4] sunrpc: protect the svc_pool_map pool_to[] array with RCU

reviews.author: NeilBrown
reviews.summary: Reviewer NeilBrown suggested an alternative solution to protect the svc_pool_map pool_to[] array by having svc_new_thread() check if the service is pooled and only call svc_pool_map_get_node() if it is, thus avoiding the need for RCU protection.
reviews.raw_body:
I don't think this is the best approach.
The problem only occurs when svc_create() is used as when
svc_create_pooled() is used, svc_pool_map_get() is called early so a
reference is held to svc_pool_map.

svc_new_thread() is the only caller of svc_pool_map_get_node(), and it
can easily check pool->sv_is_pooled and only call
svc_pool_map_get_node() is sv_is_pooled is true.  When false it should
probably use NUMA_NO_NODE.
e.g.

diff --git a/net/sunrpc/svc.c b/net/sunrpc/svc.c
index ae9ec4bf34f7..063826702f46 100644
--- a/net/sunrpc/svc.c
+++ b/net/sunrpc/svc.c
@@ -800,7 +800,10 @@ int svc_new_thread(struct svc_serv *serv, struct svc_pool *pool)
 	int node;
 	int err = 0;
 
-	node = svc_pool_map_get_node(pool->sp_id);
+	if (serv->sv_is_pooled)
+		node = svc_pool_map_get_node(pool->sp_id);
+	else
+		node = NUMA_NO_NODE;
 
 	rqstp = svc_prepare_thread(serv, pool, node);
 	if (!rqstp)


NeilBrown

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch protects the svc_pool_map pool_to[] array from concurrent access by readers and writers without proper synchronization. It uses RCU (Read-Copy Update) to ensure that readers can safely access the array even when it is being updated or torn down. The patch also updates the code to use rcu_assign_pointer() and kfree_rcu_mightsleep() to properly manage the pool_to[] array's lifetime. This fix addresses a problem where svc_pool_map_get_node() could dereference a partially built or already freed pool_to[] array, leading to potential crashes.
